steps: Go to https://www.npmjs.com and log in to your account
Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner
Select 'Access Tokens' from the dropdown menu
Enter a token name and set an expiration period
Configure package access permissions (read-only for querying, read+write for publishing)
Click 'Generate Token' and copy the token — it is shown only once

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Access Token
credential_fields.description: npm access token sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header

notes: npm is free to use for public packages; private packages require a paid plan ($7/month)
The token is shown only once at creation — copy and store it securely
Granular access tokens support fine-grained package and IP restrictions
Legacy tokens (read-only, automation, publish) are also available for simpler use cases
You can create up to 1,000 granular access tokens per account
Unauthenticated read access is available for public packages — a token is only required for publishing or accessing private packages

test_endpoint.description: Returns the username associated with the access token to verify authentication
